title: Luxury Hotel Rebrand
context: The Meridian hotel group wants a rebrand. You'll need to sort requirements AND match their business goals to the right KPIs.

requirements.text: Rebrand as accessible and affordable for budget-conscious travellers
requirements.explanation: Affordable and 5-star premium are fundamentally incompatible brand positions — you cannot credibly be both.

requirements.text: Maintain the 5-star premium positioning and luxury experience
requirements.explanation: The core brand equity is in the premium positioning — this is the foundation.

requirements.text: Create a new brand visual identity (logo, colour palette, typography)
requirements.explanation: A visual identity refresh is the core deliverable of a rebrand.

requirements.text: Use TikTok as a primary marketing channel
requirements.explanation: TikTok luxury travel content can work — several luxury brands have found success there.

requirements.text: Target both business travellers and budget backpackers equally
requirements.explanation: Business travellers and budget backpackers have opposing needs — targeting both would dilute the premium positioning.
This tagline attempts to bridge contradictory positions — it's a placeholder until the brand contradiction is resolved.

requirements.text: Update all digital touchpoints: website, app, booking flow
requirements.explanation: Digital touchpoints are core to the guest experience and must reflect the rebrand.

requirements.text: Train all front-of-house staff on the new brand standards
requirements.explanation: Brand standards training is essential for consistency but can follow the visual rollout.

goals.text: Improve brand perception among high-net-worth individuals

kpis.text: Revenue per available room (RevPAR) increase of 15%
Brand sentiment score in luxury travel surveys
Social media likes and follower count

teachingMoment: Vanity metrics like social media likes don't connect to business goals like bookings. Always tie KPIs directly to the business outcome they measure.
